local_names.chinese_simplified: 印度鹰嘴豆配油炸面包
local_names.chinese_traditional: 印度鷹嘴豆配油炸麵包

food_subgroup: Spiced chickpea curry with deep-fried leavened white bread

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Chole Bhature as a combined dish. Nearest parent is a legume curry served with fried bread. A new concept for this iconic Indian street food combination is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chole Bhature — spiced chickpea curry with deep-fried leavened bread, North Indian

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of chole bhature is 1 bhatura (approximately 150g deep-fried puffed bread) plus 1 bowl of chole (200g spiced chickpea curry), totalling approximately 350g.

nutrient_source.title: Chole Bhature — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, Entry EX000018
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match in ICMR-NIN under entry EX000018 — Chole Bhature, combined serving (200g chole + 150g bhatura).

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — estimated from combined chickpea curry (low GI) and deep-fried wheat bhatura (high GI); the combined GI is moderate but the glycaemic load per full serving is very high.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 1232mg per full serving (350g) — very high; the spice blend, salt in chole, and baking soda in bhatura all contribute.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should treat chole bhature as an occasional indulgence meal. Limit to half a serving (175g). The high potassium from chickpeas provides some offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~62) but very high glycaemic load per full serving (350g) of approximately 75 — one of the highest glycaemic loads of any standard single meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should avoid chole bhature as a regular meal. The deep-fried bhatura has a very high glycaemic load. If consumed, limit to a quarter bhatura with 100g of chole.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying oil from bhatura contributes omega-6 fatty acids; chickpeas are highly anti-inflammatory but the bhatura's refined flour and frying offset this.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Consume occasionally. The chickpea chole is anti-inflammatory and high in folate, but the deep-fried bhatura undermines the overall nutritional benefit. Prefer the chole alone with chapati.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chole Bhature is a legendary North Indian street food and comfort meal — two inseparable components served together: Chole, a dark, intensely spiced chickpea curry with a dry-ish tomato-onion gravy flavoured with dried pomegranate seed powder and whole spices; and Bhature, large, puffed, golden, deep-fried leavened bread made from refined flour and yogurt. One of the most indulgent and calorie-dense dishes in Indian cuisine, chole bhature is a beloved breakfast-to-lunch item in Delhi and Punjab. The chickpea provides outstanding nutrition (high fibre, protein, folate) but the deep-fried bhatura adds significant calories and fat.

confidence_reason: Exact match found in ICMR-NIN under entry EX000018 — Chole Bhature, combined dish.
notes: Contains gluten (bhatura is made from refined wheat flour) and dairy (yogurt in bhatura dough). Not vegan or suitable for gluten-sensitive patients. Per_100g values are for the combined chole + bhatura serving (350g total). Cholesterol approximately 8.75mg per full serving — primarily from yogurt in bhatura. Very high glycaemic load per full serving (approximately 75).
